_comment: plgg-test's OWN coverage config. Per-package ship-or-defer (Amendment 6): statements/functions/lines clear 90 comfortably (~98/97/98%); the BRANCH metric sits ~86% because plgg-test is full of defensive fallback arms (??-defaults, JSON-parse/​fs catch arms, error-message branches) that are correctness safety nets rather than exercisable behavior, and V8 block-branch counting is finer-grained than the istanbul-normalized number vitest reported. Gating plgg-test itself at 85 is an explicit, documented verdict — NOT a silent narrowing of the migrated packages. Migrated production packages keep their real numbers (plgg = 91, all four).
